mapping.a_element: iff-characterization of universal entry robustness (property holds exactly when aggregate welfare is additive)
mapping.b_element: iff-characterization of r-minimality (property holds exactly when the code induces a cutting r-blocking map / satisfies the (P,omega)-weight bound)

brief_justification: The overlap is only the generic form 'characterize a combinatorial property by a necessary-and-sufficient certificate'; the actual machinery is unrelated. Paper A uses competitive-equilibrium / LP-duality decentralization of an efficient allocation via anonymous item prices, reducing entry-robustness to welfare additivity, whereas Paper B is algebraic coding theory characterizing minimal poset-metric codewords via cutting blocking sets and Ashikhmin-Barg-type weight bounds; there is no role-for-role correspondence between prices/valuations and codewords/blocking sets.
